title: Will Lionel Messi Play Today? Latest Argentina Star Injury Update Ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026 Opener vs Algeria
original_text: Lionel Messi’s fitness has become one of the biggest talking points ahead of Argentina’s FIFA World Cup 2026 opener against Algeria. Fans were concerned after the Argentina captain picked up a leg issue during an Inter Miami CF match just weeks before the tournament.The latest Lionel Messi injury update, however, is positive for Argentina supporters. Lionel Scaloni has indicated that Messi looks fit and is expected to start in tonight’s Group J clash as Argentina begins its World Cup title defense.Why There Were Injury Concerns Over Lionel MessiInjury concerns around Lionel Messi began in May 2026 when the Inter Miami CF star appeared uncomfortable during a match against the Philadelphia Union. Messi was seen holding the back of his left leg before asking to be substituted in the 73rd minute, quickly raising concerns ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026.Medical tests later showed Messi suffered a mild hamstring overload linked to muscle fatigue rather than a serious injury. Officials also clarified that taking him off the field was a precaution, especially because of the difficult conditions on a rain-soaked pitch.The timing of the issue increased worries among Argentina fans, especially with the World Cup only weeks away. Any fitness concern involving Messi usually draws major attention because of his importance to Argentina’s squad.Is Lionel Messi Expected to Start Against Algeria?Yes, Lionel Messi is expected to start for Argentina against Algeria tonight. Argentina head coach Lionel Scaloni has suggested the captain is fit and ready to lead the defending champions in their opening Group J match.The Argentina vs Algeria clash takes place on June 16 in Kansas City and marks the official start of Argentina’s FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign. Messi’s expected return to the starting lineup is a major boost for a team aiming to defend its title.Several reports from Argentina’s camp also suggest Messi is likely to feature from the opening whistle, easing fears about his availability before kickoff.What Lionel Scaloni Has Said About Messi's FitnessLionel Scaloni has remained optimistic about Messi’s recovery throughout the past few weeks. Shortly after the injury concern emerged, the Argentina manager reassured fans and said the situation “is not that bad,” while stressing the importance of careful recovery.As Messi gradually returned to full training, Scaloni revealed that the forward was “doing well” and successfully rejoined team sessions. Argentina’s coaching staff also carefully managed his minutes in a warm-up match against Iceland, where Messi played around 20 minutes without discomfort.Speaking ahead of the Algeria game, Scaloni offered another encouraging update by saying, “Leo has always been there and has always been fundamental... and he looks good.” He also acknowledged Messi’s global popularity, adding that “the whole planet wants to see him play.”Predicted Argentina Lineup vs AlgeriaArgentina is expected to line up in a 4-3-3 formation against Algeria, although there could still be a decision to make at left-back.Predicted Argentina XI: Emiliano Martínez; Nahuel Molina, Cristian Romero, Nicolás Otamendi, Facundo Medina or Nicolás Tagliafico; Rodrigo De Paul, Enzo Fernández, Alexis Mac Allister; Lionel Messi, Lautaro Martínez or Julián Álvarez, Thiago Almada.What Messi's Availability Means for ArgentinaMessi’s availability gives Argentina a major boost heading into the FIFA World Cup 2026. His presence adds creativity in attack and often forces opponents to focus heavily on stopping him, opening spaces for teammates in dangerous areas.Beyond tactics, Messi’s expected involvement also lifts confidence inside the squad as Argentina begins its title defense. If he takes the field against Algeria, the captain will become the first footballer to appear in six FIFA World Cups, adding another historic milestone to his legendary career.Also visit: FIFA World Cup 2026 schedule FIFA Points Table 2026Get the latest Sports News and Live updates.
